OpenAI releases GPT-X: a leap forward in reasoning

By Jane Reporter — April 15, 2026

OpenAI on Monday unveiled GPT-X, its newest large language model, claiming significant gains in multi-step reasoning and tool use over the previous generation. The model is available immediately through the API at a reduced price point targeted at high-volume developers.

Early benchmarks published by the company show GPT-X solving olympiad-level mathematics problems that previously required agentic scaffolding, and doing so in a single forward pass. Independent evaluations are still pending and are expected later this week from third-party labs.

Share

Continue reading

The release comes amid growing competition from rival labs, several of whom have shipped comparable models in the past month. Analysts expect the pricing pressure to accelerate commoditization of the frontier-model market, a trend OpenAI has publicly said it welcomes.

Advertisement

In a briefing with reporters, the company said GPT-X was trained with a new reinforcement-learning regime that explicitly rewards self-critique. "We want the model to notice when it is wrong and try again," a spokesperson said.
